title: peplis nummulariifolia var. boraei
intro: Peplis nummulariifolia var. boraei, also known as Boraea Pepli, is a plant species that belongs to the family Tiliaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Africa and Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic qualities. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, and is also used to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and rheumatism. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Peplis nummulariifolia var. boraei are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or used in traditional medicines to harness its medicinal properties.
